growth_records.cite: Chesneau, Guillaume et al. Lactuchelins represent lipopeptide siderophores produced by Pseudomonas lactucae that inhibit Xanthomonas campestris. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag003. [PMC12904278]
growth_records.snippet: CFS from this coculture decreased the growth rate (0.31 h-1, 35% reduction) and maximal population density of Xcc8004 (0.38, 30% reduction), while increased the time to reach half of the maximum density by 2 h
growth_records.notes: This is a growth-INHIBITION/competition assay, not a metabolic (GEM/MFA) characterization; captured because it is the only numeric prokaryote growth rate in the paper. Measured mu = 0.31 h-1 for Xcc8004 grown in TSB10% + cell-free supernatant of a P. lactucae/Xcc8004 co-culture, 28 C. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.31 = 2.24 h (calculated). The 0.31 h-1 is a 35% reduction vs the reference (P. lactucae-alone CFS) condition; implied uninhibited baseline = 0.31/(1-0.35) = 0.48 h-1 (derived, not directly reported). Medium is a complex undefined broth (TSB), so composition not tabulated. No uptake/secretion rates measured.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 17596 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/17596

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test

phenotype_groups.label: Complex/peptone base (acid-production test)
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
